pi_items.source: Task setup script (setup_task.sh) confirms this path; standard Linux desktop location
Reasonable minimum for a technical report with 4 sections; verifier.py enforces this threshold
Standard section for technical infrastructure assessments; matches feasibility report conventions
Standard section for technical architecture documentation; verified in enterprise integration guides
Standard final section of feasibility reports; matches EHR integration assessment standards
Standard section for technical investigation results; validates empirical observations
OMG Data Distribution Service - core middleware standard used by OpenICE. Verified via https://github.com/mdpnp/mdpnp documentation and OMG specifications
DDS is a middleware technology; RTI Connext DDS is the specific middleware implementation in OpenICE
Core DDS concept - topics are application-selected labels used to identify publications and subscriptions in DDS
Core DDS concept - a DDS domain is a concept used to bind individual applications together for communication
Core DDS concept - participants are entities within a domain that contain local endpoints (DataWriters and DataReaders)
DDS implements publish-subscribe pattern; publishers create topics and publish data samples
DDS publish-subscribe pattern - subscribers listen to topics via DataReaders
Real-time Publish-Subscribe (RTPS) protocol - OMG standard interoperability protocol for DDS, used in OpenICE
Interface Description Language - used in OpenICE to define data types and structures (ice.idl file in mdpnp repository)
Real data structure from OpenICE ice.idl - represents sensor observations at slow rates (≤3Hz) with device, metric, and value fields
Real data structure from OpenICE ice.idl - designed for high-rate sensor data (>3Hz) with frequency and array of values
Real data structure from OpenICE ice.idl - represents infusion pump state (deprecated but documented); includes drug info and progress metrics
Real data structure from OpenICE ice.idl - defines threshold values for device alarms with limit type and numeric threshold
Used in ICE data models to identify measured quantities; key field in Numeric, SampleArray, and AlarmLimit
Used in ICE data models to differentiate multiple instances of the same metric on a device
Health Level Seven International standard for healthcare data exchange; widely used for EHR integration
HL7 Fast Healthcare Interoperability Resources - modern standard for EHR data exchange and interoperability
Mirth Connect - open-source healthcare integration engine used for medical device and EHR integration; supports HL7/FHIR
Standard healthcare integration term for connections between systems
Integration architecture component; Mirth Connect can serve as an information gateway for healthcare systems
OpenICE device-adapters translate device protocols into standard ICE data structures; core component of platform
Application Programming Interface - standard method for system integration and data exchange
Core healthcare integration goal; DDS and RTPS protocols ensure interoperability between different vendor implementations
Standard healthcare term; EHR systems are the target for OpenICE medical device integration
Multiparameter Monitor - standard ICU device; OpenICE supports simulation and adaptation of multiparameter monitors
Medical device for measuring oxygen saturation; OpenICE includes multiple pulse oximeter device adapters (Nonin protocol support documented)
Medical device for monitoring end-tidal CO2; standard ICU monitoring device supported by medical device platforms
Medical device for controlled drug/fluid delivery; OpenICE has InfusionStatus data model and infusion pump simulation support
Cardiac monitoring device; OpenICE includes simulated ECG device in devices/simulated/src/main/java/org/mdpnp/devices/simulation/ecg
Oxygen saturation measurement parameter; core vital sign measured by pulse oximeters and multiparameter monitors in ICU settings

privileged_info_summary: This task investigates OpenICE (MD PnP Open-source Integrated Clinical Environment), a real OMG DDS-based medical device interoperability platform. All metadata claims have been verified: (1) DDS architectural terms (topic, domain, participant, publish-subscribe, RTPS, IDL) are authentic OMG standards used in OpenICE; (2) ICE data models (Numeric, SampleArray, InfusionStatus, AlarmLimit) are real structures from the ice.idl specification in the mdpnp repository; (3) integration keywords (HL7, FHIR, Mirth) reference genuine healthcare standards and tools used for EHR integration; (4) device types (Multiparameter Monitor, Pulse Oximeter, Capnograph, Infusion Pump, ECG, SpO2) are real medical devices supported by OpenICE simulators; (5) required report sections follow standard technical feasibility assessment structure; (6) 500-byte minimum is reasonable for the specified 4-section technical report. The task uses synthetic simulated device data, not real patient information.
